reviews.title: Truly the best earbuds out there...
reviews.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on October 22, 2025
reviews.body: I use these earbuds while mowing on the farm—both on a tractor and a riding lawn mower—and I’m genuinely impressed. With my Apple AirPods, I repeatedly struggled to keep them in once I got hot and sweaty, which affected both sound quality and volume. Not so with these.I’ve mowed twice in the Texas heat, and they’ve stayed securely in place the entire time. The sound quality remains consistent, with noticeably better bass and effective noise cancellation that blocks out the roar of the equipment. These earbuds have made a real difference in my workday comfort and listening experience.
reviews.highlight_phrase: stayed securely in place the entire time

reviews.title: Stay away. Do not waste your money.
reviews.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on November 14, 2025
reviews.variant_text: Color: WhiteStyle: Buds 3 ProPattern: Buds 3 Pro Only
reviews.body: Stay away. Find something else that will actually work. I had the Galaxy Buds for over 5yrs. Absolute favorite. Lasted well over 8hrs until this year when the left one started losing battery retention. I figure Ingot my use out of them and they performed flawlessly and I am probably rough on them as in dirt, weather conditions,  wear and tear, and they never failed like these new ones. Never. I looked up the newest buds and found these were "supposed" to be it. Worst decision. When, that's if you can get them to charge, you are lucky to get 3 sometimes if you never receive a call, sometimes 4hrs. Then IF, and that's a big if, you will be lucky to get them to charge. They are in their factory case all day, put them in, 7% battery please charge. What, are you kidding me. I have had to "reset" them 4 times already. They will lose their connection with any attached device. They don't fit in my ears at all. I had to get the earbud hooks to keep them in. Even with those, I suggest you not talk,eat, or move your head or they will just fall out. Never again. Samsung entirely made me rethink Apple products with these. Sad day for Samsung.
reviews.highlight_phrase: not talk,eat, or move your head

reviews.title: Good but for money..
reviews.date_text: Reviewed in the United States on March 27, 2025
reviews.variant_text: Color: Electric OrangeStyle: Powerbeats Pro 2Set: Without AppleCare+
reviews.body: *Back 8/2025 to drop another star.**These headphones are really troublesome. My left bud constantly dies in the case. If you leave head detection on - these things are constantly connected in the case. You have to factory reset - reconnect them - forget them - all for that same bud to get stuck charging at 65%. And any sort of smiling or facial use - will drive these to hurt in your ears - if you trust their proper fit guide and likely use the largest size you can stomach. Just so expensive for same annoyances with these types of design. And if you sweat in these they constantly rotate and you lose half the sound quality. I just lose appeal. I have gone full weeks not even wanting to wear these. Its very strange to have the most premium of earbuds - and to just look at them with regret....Just to get to the point - do these headphones sound good. Sure. Do they sound like they are worth the premium money they are asking for. No. No way.I have JBL headphones $50~ that with a $5 app you can get to pound like these headphones. Im no audiophile, but yes these headphones dont seem to crackle, and have as many deadspots in certain frequencies. there is a brightness to these - so you can feel some of that money you spend...but at the end of the day I dont think a $100 cheaper pair is going to be distinguishable.And I think the biggest sin of these, is use your common sense. If you have disliked in ear headphones in the past - these are using the same technology. The buds into the ear are the same design as the 20$ pairs. Theres not enough innovation here to get me to buy the price was worth it.They have good sound and spatially you can hear some 360 type audio from them. But most things dont use that to full effect anyway so to pay 2 to 3 to 10 times the price.....I cant recommend the splurge.I will say they feel weightless the way they are supported. But ergonomically if how your ear holes are presented these are not aligned - they are going to hurt. For me I have to wear them with biggest size rubbers and if i smile or talk - they start to walk out. But if I wear the lesser sized ones you can feel the depth of sound loss going from XL XXL down to the regular earbuds they come installed with.The case also feels like the tops will eventually be crushed as well. Like a squeaky bed. If you squeeze the case / grab it between thumb and forefinger - it squeaks and that back hinge is going to break at some point. i have no doubt. Not chintzy but for 2.5 big bills I want bombproof.If you just chill out at home get some open air cans, if you want to go running and have a nice set these will do - but why risk losing these pair or them getting wet. This design is in $5 headphones and any headphone with some bass gain will be just fine for a workout.This was a splurge for me and I do regret it. But I wanted to give them time and thats over any return period. They just are good enough to go "overpaid" but not "I was robbed".
reviews.highlight_phrase: sweat in these they constantly rotate
